title: Getting started with stomachr
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("maxlindmark/stomachr")

title: Getting started with stomachr
text: can download data portal go Step 2. can call download_stomach(), downloads data ICES API writes four CSVs local directory (control via path). Ecoregion filtering supported API (actual data seems!), can filter geographically joining using lat/lon ices_rectangle. restrict specific years countries: vignette run pipeline raw CSVs North Sea (2020-2024) bundled package inst/extdata/, downloaded .
code: path <- "data/raw" download_stomach(path) download_stomach(path, year = 2000:2010, country = c("DK", "NO", "SE")) library(stomachr) path <- system.file("extdata", package = "stomachr")

title: Getting started with stomachr
text: next step join four CSVs. means attaching haul file metadata predators, classifying stomach "food", "empty", "unidentified", deduplicates exact-duplicate prey rows (present national submissions), converts prey length mm cm, handles count sentinel value (9999 = unknown multiplicity), optionally imputes missing coordinates ICES rectangle midpoints (defaults yes). Known country-specific unit issues (currently requires manual correction proceeding): Belgium (): pred_length mm – divide 10 Denmark (DK): ind_wgt kg – multiply 1000
code: dat <- join_stomach_data(path) #> join_stomach_data(): 9275 predator individuals #> ✔ 3932 with identifiable prey #> ℹ 4253 empty or regurgitated #> ℹ 1090 with prey records but no prey species ID #>   (cannot contribute to diet composition but can contribute to total prey #>   weight) #> ℹ 0 haul locations imputed from ICES rectangle midpoint  dat <- dat |>   dplyr::mutate(     pred_length = dplyr::if_else(country == "BE", pred_length / 10, pred_length),     ind_wgt     = dplyr::if_else(country == "DK", ind_wgt * 1000, ind_wgt)   )

title: Getting started with stomachr
text: Removes predators regurgitated >= 1. Stomach contents regurgitated fish techically usable. na_regurgitated argument controls whether NA values treated regurgitated ("keep", default) regurgitated ("drop").
code: dat <- drop_invalid(dat, na_regurgitated = "keep") #> drop_invalid(): 9275 -> 8988 predators (287 dropped, 3.1%) #> ℹ regurgitated >= 1 assumed regurgitated #> ℹ regurgitated == NA assumed not regurgitated (n = 4427 kept) #> ℹ Dropped by country: #>   country   n percent_of_total #> 1      BE   7             0.1% #> 2      DK   7             0.1% #> 3      NL 114             1.2% #> 4      SE 159             1.7%

title: Getting started with stomachr
text: Joins scientific names higher taxonomy (class, order, family, phylum) predators prey internal WoRMS lookup. Prey aphia_id_prey = NA non-empty stomachs labelled "Unknown" weight silently lost.
code: dat <- add_taxonomy(dat) #> add_taxonomy(): WoRMS names resolved #> ✔ Predator AphiaIDs: 23 unique, 0 unresolved #> ✔ Prey AphiaIDs: 252 unique, 0 unresolved

title: Getting started with stomachr
text: Estimates missing prey weight /length via L/W parameters observed means, estimates missing predator weight length. Also creates final predator_weight column (observed weight available, otherwise estimated length). L/W parameters looked internal table (FishBase fish, Robinson 2010 invertebrates) hierarchical fallback: species -> family -> order -> class -> phylum -> universal (= 0.01, b = 3). : impute "prey", "pred", "" (default) method: "lw_params" (default) uses bundled L/W table; "observations" uses mean sizes records species size: impute "weight", "length", "" (default) fill_if_no_size: TRUE (default), prey recorded size borrow records species applying L/W
code: dat <- impute_size(dat, which = "both", method = "lw_params", size = "both") #> impute_size(): which = "both" | method = "lw_params" | size = "both" | #> fill_if_no_size = TRUE #> Prey: 8169 records | L/W params (unique AphiaIDs): species: 64, family: 40, #> order: 31, class: 57, phylum: 16, universal (a=0.01, b=3): 44 #> |-- both weight and length recorded: 1871 (22.9%) #> |-- one size recorded, other estimated: 6207 (76.0%) #> | |-- had length, estimated weight via L/W: 21 (0.3%) #> | +-- had weight, estimated length via L/W: 6186 (75.7%) #> |-- no size recorded, imputed from other records: 88 (1.1%) #> | |-- same stomach: mean weight -> length via L/W: 8 (0.1%) #> | |-- same pred-prey pair: mean weight -> length via L/W: 77 (0.9%) #> | +-- global species mean: mean weight -> length via L/W: 3 (0.0%) #> +-- no size info in any record of that species: 3 (0.0%) (diet composition #> only, weight unusable) #> Predator: 14562 rows | L/W params (unique AphiaIDs): species: 22, family: 1 #> |-- weight and length observed: 14562 (100.0%) #> +-- weight observed, length missing: 0 (0.0%)

title: Getting started with stomachr
text: Returns analysis-ready columns.
code: dat <- trim_data(dat) #> trim_data(): dropped 33 columns: #>   ship, gear, haul_no, station_number, fish_id, ind_wgt, number, #>   measurement_increment, code, maturity_scale, maturity_stage, #>   preservation_method, stomach_fullness, full_stom_wgt, empty_stom_wgt, #>   stomach_empty, gen_samp, notes, ident_met, grav_method, prey_sequence, #>   unit_wgt, weight, unit_lngt, other_items, other_count, predator_rank, #>   predator_phylum, predator_genus, prey_rank, prey_phylum, prey_genus, #>   ind_weight_est

title: Getting started with stomachr
text: sense_check() adds sense_flag column marking implausible records. drop_flagged() removes . can inspect flagged records dropping examining tbl_predator_information_id raw data.
code: dat <- sense_check(dat) #> sense_check(): 14562 rows #> ! total stomach content heavier than predator: 1 row (0.0%) across 1 predator #>   tbl_predator_information_id: 110348 #> ℹ 1 row flagged (0.01%) #> ℹ Use `drop_flagged()` to remove, or inspect tbl_predator_information_id in raw #>   data dat <- drop_flagged(dat) #> ✔ drop_flagged(): removed 1 row (0.01%), 14561 remaining

title: Getting started with stomachr
text: next sections show basic plots common predators build North Sea data (2020–2024).
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(tidyr) library(forcats) library(scales)  theme_set(theme_light()) n <- 10  top <- dat |>   distinct(tbl_predator_information_id, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   count(predator_scientific_name, sort = TRUE) |>   slice_head(n = n) |>   pull(predator_scientific_name)

title: Getting started with stomachr
code: plot_map(dat, color = "year")

what: Proportion of empty, food, and unidentified stomachs by year and predators
title: Getting started with stomachr
text: plot, use distinct() get one row per predator, count frequency stomach-statuses across years predator species:
code: dat |>   filter(predator_scientific_name %in% top) |>   distinct(tbl_predator_information_id, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   count(year, predator_scientific_name, stomach_status) |>   mutate(frac = n / sum(n), .by = c(year, predator_scientific_name)) |>   ggplot(aes(year, frac, fill = stomach_status)) +   geom_col(alpha = 0.9) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = \(x) pretty(x, n = 3)) +   facet_wrap(~predator_scientific_name, ncol = 3) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Percent", fill = "Stomach status")

title: Getting started with stomachr
text: plot predator-prey mass ratios, can use final dataset, need uncount(count), one row one individual prey!
code: dat |>   filter(predator_scientific_name %in% top, stomach_status == "food") |>   drop_na(predator_weight, prey_weight_ind) |>   drop_na(prey_class) |>    uncount(count) |>   ggplot(aes(predator_weight, prey_weight_ind, color = prey_class)) +   facet_wrap(~predator_scientific_name, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +   scale_color_viridis_d() +   scale_x_log10() +   scale_y_log10() +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, alpha = 1))) +   labs(x = "Predator weight (g)", y = "Prey weight (g)", color = "Prey class") +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Getting started with stomachr
code: plot_dat <- dat |>   filter(     predator_scientific_name %in% top,     stomach_status == "food",     !is.na(prey_weight_all_ind)   ) |>   mutate(     prey_group = fct_lump_n(coalesce(prey_class, "Other"), n = 5, w = prey_weight_all_ind, other_level = "Other"),     n_pred = n_distinct(tbl_predator_information_id), .by = predator_scientific_name   ) |>   summarise(     total_weight = sum(prey_weight_all_ind),     n_pred = first(n_pred),     .by = c(predator_scientific_name, prey_group)   ) |>   mutate(frac = total_weight / sum(total_weight), .by = predator_scientific_name)  ggplot(plot_dat, aes(frac, predator_scientific_name, fill = prey_group)) +   geom_col() +   geom_text(     data = distinct(plot_dat, predator_scientific_name, n_pred),     aes(x = 1.01, y = predator_scientific_name, label = n_pred),     hjust = 0, inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3   ) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Spectral") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.08), expand = c(0, 0)) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE, clip = "off") +   labs(x = "Diet proportion (by weight)", y = NULL, fill = "Prey class")

what: Cod diet over time by prey family
title: Getting started with stomachr
code: dat |>   filter(     predator_scientific_name == "Gadus morhua",     stomach_status == "food",     !is.na(prey_weight_all_ind)   ) |>   mutate(prey_group = fct_lump_n(prey_family, n = n, w = prey_weight_all_ind, other_level = "Other")) |>   summarise(total_weight = sum(prey_weight_all_ind), .by = c(year, prey_group)) |>   mutate(frac = total_weight / sum(total_weight), .by = year) |>   ggplot(aes(year, frac, fill = prey_group)) +   geom_col() +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Spectral", na.value = "grey80") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = \(x) pretty(x, n = 6)) +   scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +   labs(     x = "Year", y = "Diet proportion (by weight)", fill = "Prey family",     title = "Gadus morhua"   )

title: Getting started with stomachr

what: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
title: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
text: Downloading cleaning ICES stomach content data stomachr package downloads ICES stomach content data provides tools merging, enriching, imputing, trimming, quality-checking filtering stomach data ICES stomach database. ’s meant complement FishStomachs R package, working directly new ICES stomach database new format, well focusing actual data preparation general use, rather preparing aggregated stomachs SMS model. idea lower threshold get started analyzing stomach content data (can overwhelming work ) presenting structured reproducible workflow. Inspired tidylog, prints clean summaries operation, flags unusual observations easily get overview happens processing scripts. full pipeline produces single tibble, one row per prey record per predator, suitable analysis diet composition, food levels, predator-prey mass ratios, ! See walkthrough!

title: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
text: also build vignette locally, change install command:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("maxlindmark/stomachr") remotes::install_github("maxlindmark/stomachr", build_vignettes = TRUE)

title: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
text: use functions package preparing data, can either download data ICES portal put designated path. can use built download_stomach function:

title: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
text: Filter year country:
code: library(stomachr)  download_stomach(path = "data/raw") download_stomach(path = "data/raw", year = 2000:2010, country = c("DK", "NO", "SE"))

title: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
text: ICES stomach data come new format four related CSV files: Species identified AphiaID (WoRMS taxonomic identifiers). stomachr resolves scientific names higher taxonomy using bundled lookup table built WoRMS API, user .

title: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
text: key functions package, takes four raw csv something ready analysing, :

title: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
text: four raw North Sea CSV files (2020-2024) bundled inst/extdata/ used vignette("stomachr") demonstrate full pipeline.

title: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
text: full pipeline may look like :
code: dat <- join_stomach_data("data/raw") |>   drop_invalid() |>   add_taxonomy()|>   impute_size() |>   trim_data() |>   sense_check() |>   drop_flagged()

title: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data
text: full walkthrough pipeline example analyses (diet composition, predator–prey mass ratios, temporal trends), see:
code: vignette("stomachr")

what: Add WoRMS taxonomy to predator and prey — add_taxonomy
title: Add WoRMS taxonomy to predator and prey — add_taxonomy
text: Left-joins scientific names higher taxonomy (class, order, family, phylum) predators prey using bundled WoRMS lookup table (worms_lookup, built data-raw/build_worms_lookup.R). Prey aphia_id_prey = NA non-empty stomachs labelled "Unknown" weight silently lost downstream.

title: Add WoRMS taxonomy to predator and prey — add_taxonomy
code: add_taxonomy(dat)

title: Add WoRMS taxonomy to predator and prey — add_taxonomy
text: dat Tibble drop_invalid().

title: Add WoRMS taxonomy to predator and prey — add_taxonomy
text: dat added columns predator_scientific_name, predator_class, predator_order, predator_family, predator_phylum, matching prey_* columns.

what: Download ICES stomach content data — download_stomach
title: Download ICES stomach content data — download_stomach
text: Downloads four ICES stomach content CSVs (File_information.csv, HaulInformation.csv, PredatorInformation.csv, PreyInformation.csv) stomachdata.ices.dk writes path.

title: Download ICES stomach content data — download_stomach
code: download_stomach(   path = ".",   year = NULL,   country = NULL,   reporting_org = NULL,   cruise_id = NULL )

title: Download ICES stomach content data — download_stomach
text: path Directory write four CSV files . Created exist. Defaults current working directory. year Integer vector years, e.g. 2000:2010 c(2005, 2010). country Character vector ISO country codes, e.g. c("DK", ""). reporting_org Character vector reporting organisation names. cruise_id Character vector cruise IDs retain.

title: Download ICES stomach content data — download_stomach
text: path, invisibly.

title: Download ICES stomach content data — download_stomach
text: year country applied server-side (one request per combination). reporting_org cruise_id applied locally download. Omitting filters downloads full database single request. Ecoregion filtering supported: ICES API ignores ecoregion parameters downloaded CSVs contain ecoregion column. Filter geographically join_stomach_data() using lat/lon ices_rectangle.

title: Remove flagged records — drop_flagged
text: Removes rows sense_flag NA (.e., rows flagged sense_check()).

title: Remove flagged records — drop_flagged
code: drop_flagged(dat)

title: Remove flagged records — drop_flagged
text: dat Tibble sense_check().

title: Remove flagged records — drop_flagged
text: dat flagged rows removed.

title: Drop invalid (regurgitated) predator records — drop_invalid
text: Removes predators regurgitated >= 1. Stomach contents regurgitated fish scientifically unusable diet analysis.

title: Drop invalid (regurgitated) predator records — drop_invalid
code: drop_invalid(dat, na_regurgitated = "keep")

title: Drop invalid (regurgitated) predator records — drop_invalid
text: dat Tibble join_stomach_data(). na_regurgitated "keep" (default) treats NA regurgitated; "drop" treats NA regurgitated.

title: Drop invalid (regurgitated) predator records — drop_invalid
text: dat regurgitated predators removed.

what: Impute missing prey and predator sizes — impute_size
title: Impute missing prey and predator sizes — impute_size
text: Estimates missing prey weight /length via L/W parameters observed means, estimates missing predator weight length.

title: Impute missing prey and predator sizes — impute_size
code: impute_size(   dat,   which = c("both", "prey", "pred"),   method = c("lw_params", "observations"),   size = c("both", "weight", "length"),   fill_if_no_size = TRUE )

title: Impute missing prey and predator sizes — impute_size
text: dat Tibble add_taxonomy(). One "" (default), "prey", "pred". method One "lw_params" (default) "observations". size One "" (default), "weight", "length". fill_if_no_size TRUE (default), prey records neither weight length borrow size stomach, predator-prey pair, global species mean, apply L/W.

title: Impute missing prey and predator sizes — impute_size
text: dat imputed size columns prey_lw_source / pred_lw_source provenance columns. Internal L/W parameter columns dropped.

what: Read and join the four ICES stomach content CSVs — join_stomach_data
title: Read and join the four ICES stomach content CSVs — join_stomach_data
text: Reads File_information.csv, HaulInformation.csv, PredatorInformation.csv, PreyInformation.csv path, joins single flat tibble, classifies stomach "food", "empty", "unidentified", deduplicates exact-duplicate prey rows, optionally imputes missing coordinates ICES rectangle midpoints.

title: Read and join the four ICES stomach content CSVs — join_stomach_data
code: join_stomach_data(path, impute_coords = TRUE)

title: Read and join the four ICES stomach content CSVs — join_stomach_data
text: path Path directory containing four ICES CSV files. impute_coords TRUE (default), missing lat/lon imputed ICES rectangle midpoint via mapplots::ices.rect().

title: Read and join the four ICES stomach content CSVs — join_stomach_data
text: tibble one row per prey record per predator. Empty unidentified stomachs contribute one NA prey row .

title: Length-weight parameters — lw_params
text: L/W parameters prey predator species, taxonomic fallback rows family, order, class, phylum level. Built data-raw/build_lw_params.R.

title: Length-weight parameters — lw_params
code: lw_params

title: Length-weight parameters — lw_params
text: tibble columns: aphia_id WoRMS AphiaID (species-level rows ) family, order, class, phylum Taxonomic group (fallback rows) lw_a Coefficient \(\) \(W = \cdot L^b\) lw_b Exponent \(b\) lw_source One "species", "family", "order", "class", "phylum"

title: Length-weight parameters — lw_params
text: Formula: \(W_g = \cdot L_{cm}^b\) Sources: Fish (Chordata): FishBase via rfishbase::length_weight(), averaged across parameter sets log10 scale. Invertebrates: Robinson et al. (2010), originally mm, rescaled cm.

title: Length-weight parameters — lw_params
text: Robinson, R.. et al. (2010). Trophic relationships marine benthic invertebrates North Sea. Journal Marine Biological Association United Kingdom, 90(7), 1375-1388.

what: Plot sampling locations on a map — plot_map
title: Plot sampling locations on a map — plot_map
text: Plots haul locations points Lambert Conformal Conic projection centred North Sea.

title: Plot sampling locations on a map — plot_map
code: plot_map(   dat,   color = "predator_scientific_name",   facet = "predator_scientific_name",   species = NULL,   ncol = 4 )

title: Plot sampling locations on a map — plot_map
text: dat Tibble trim_data() later. Must contain columns lat, lon, tbl_predator_information_id. color Column name (string) colour points . Defaults "predator_scientific_name". facet Column name (string) facet . Defaults "predator_scientific_name". species Character vector species include (filters predator_scientific_name). Defaults top 8 predators number stomachs. ncol Number columns facet_wrap.

title: Plot sampling locations on a map — plot_map
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot sampling locations on a map — plot_map
text: Requires rnaturalearth package background land layer.

title: Flag implausible records — sense_check
text: Adds sense_flag column (pipe-separated string active checks) mark rows fail following checks:

title: Flag implausible records — sense_check
code: sense_check(dat)

title: Flag implausible records — sense_check
text: dat Tibble trim_data().

title: Flag implausible records — sense_check
text: dat added sense_flag column (NA = flag).

title: Flag implausible records — sense_check
text: prey_length: prey longer predator prey_weight: individual prey heavier predator stomach_weight: total stomach content weight exceeds predator weight pred_length: predator length <= 0 >= 999 (sentinel value) count_censored: count 9999 sentinel (unknown multiplicity) coord_outlier: lat/lon outside plausible bounds covering North Sea, Baltic, Celtic Sea region (lat 45-72, lon -20 30)

what: stomachr: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data — stomachr-package
title: stomachr: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data — stomachr-package
text: tidy pipeline downloading cleaning ICES stomach content data four-table relational schema used ICES stomach content database (stomachdata.ices.dk). Produces single flat tibble suitable individual-level diet analysis.

title: stomachr: Download and Clean ICES Stomach Content Data — stomachr-package
text: Maintainer: Max Lindmark max.lindmark@slu.se

title: Trim to analysis-ready columns — trim_data
text: Drops internal processing columns longer needed cleaning pipeline, keeping columns listed .

title: Trim to analysis-ready columns — trim_data
code: trim_data(dat)

title: Trim to analysis-ready columns — trim_data
text: dat Tibble impute_size().

title: Trim to analysis-ready columns — trim_data
text: tibble columns: tbl_upload_id, tbl_haul_id, tbl_predator_information_id, country, survey, year, month, day, time, lat, lon, ices_rectangle, depth, stomach_status, regurgitated, aphia_id_predator, predator_scientific_name, predator_class, predator_order, predator_family, pred_length, predator_weight, predator_weight_estimated, age, sex, tbl_prey_information_id, aphia_id_prey, prey_scientific_name, prey_class, prey_order, prey_family, digestion_stage, sub_factor, count, count_censored, prey_length, prey_weight_ind, prey_weight_all_ind, other_wgt, prey_lw_source, lw_source, pred_lw_source, analysing_org.

title: WoRMS taxonomic lookup table — worms_lookup
text: Scientific names higher taxonomy every AphiaID appears full ICES stomach content database. Built data-raw/build_worms_lookup.R via WoRMS API (worrms::wm_record()).

title: WoRMS taxonomic lookup table — worms_lookup
code: worms_lookup

title: WoRMS taxonomic lookup table — worms_lookup
text: tibble columns: aphia_id WoRMS AphiaID (integer) scientific_name Scientific name rank Taxonomic rank (e.g. "Species", "Genus") phylum Phylum class Class order Order family Family genus Genus
